subject: LU-3326 tests: sync after file deleted in run_*.sh

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

sorry, I don't understand the issue. if the client has dirty data, then there must be enough space to complete I/O with no need to destroy any object.

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 1: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/e362c880-3e43-11e3-92d2-52540035b04c).  Ran 17 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1:

1, After client complete the unlink of some file, MDT will send object destroy requests to OSTs asynchronously, the delay is about several seconds

2, At client, the dirty data of the unlinked file will be flushed to OST after the unlink (these writes will also consume the disk space)

3, When OFD receive the object destroy request, one LDLM extent lock (0 ~ OBD_OBJECT_EOF) will be enqueued and it will tell clients to discard dirty data.

the main purpose of this patch is fixing the issue caused by the delay of the object destroy.

Patch Set 1:

I still don't follow, the space used for the objects to be destroy isn't granted to anyone until it's released by OST. so what's the issue?

Patch Set 1:

the cached pages of unlinked objects at Clients are not taken into account when calculating the available disk space, then the disk space would be not enough
when writing the new file.

in this case(dd in a loop), the free disk space is sometimes not big for there could be some objects without receiving the destroy request sent by MDT,
then the free disk space won't be enough to allow the total disk requirement of the new "dd" and the unflushed (cached) pages of unlinked objects.

this issue can also be fixed by modifying the run_dd.sh to wait enough disk space (free_disk_space - possible_cached_pages > 0)

Patch Set 1:

> this issue can also be fixed by modifying the run_dd.sh to wait enough disk space (free_disk_space - possible_cached_pages > 0)

this + explicit exra sync in the test should be correct solution to the problem.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(4 inline comments)

I think it still isn't clear why the DISCARD_DATA flag itself is not solving this problem. It also seems like there is some strangeness with the passed flags that needs to be understood.

Patch Set 1:

I think it is a bug in the grant code if the cached space of an unlinked file is not accounted in the grant.

Patch Set 5:

> Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/a5318e00-e043-11e3-80f8-52540035b04c). Ran 5 tests. 1 tests failed: recovery-mds-scale.

The test_failover_ost passed in 8 hours with OST failed for 24 times. The test_failover_mds was run for 4 hours with MDS failed for 12 times and stopped because test node was not booted up.

The two sub-tests did not hit out of space failure.

Patch Set 5:

> at the moment I think it's better to wait for space just before specific test, otherwise we'd have to ensure every test should leave enough space for a subsequent one?

In run_*.sh, the operations are run in a loop to create and delete file. E.g., in run_dd.sh:

while [ ! -e "$END_RUN_FILE" ] && $CONTINUE; do
        #......
        mkdir -p $TESTDIR
        dd bs=4k count=$BLKS status=noxfer if=/dev/zero of=$TESTDIR/dd-file
        rm -rf $TESTDIR
        #......
done

So, in order to ensure $TESTDIR/dd-file is deleted, we need call wait_delete_completed after "rm -rf $TESTDIR".

Patch Set 6:

(2 comments)

I'm concerned that adding wait_delete_completed() to all of the test scripts will delay these tests running for a long time and make the . If there are deletes happening on all of the other test nodes, then all of the scripts will pause until all of the deletes are finished, and there will hardly be an IO going to the filesystem. 

Is there some way for you to see how many of these jobs are run in a previous test and how many are run in a test with this patch?  It would be a lot better to just have the MDS destroy objects more aggressively if the filesystem is getting full. 

Alternately, would it be possible to just increase the OST size for these tests so that there do not need to be hacks like this in the test script?
Uploaded patch set 7.

commit.subject: LU-3326 tests: sync after file deleted in run_*.sh
commit.message:
LU-3326 tests: sync after file deleted in run_*.sh

In run_*.sh, call sync to flush any dirty pages to OST
if MDT has not sent the object destroy request to it,
which will shrink the available disk space gotten before
running the tests and cause -EIO error.

Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ia246122a5c3471b7cbea9cdaa7fad2ea4db856c5
Test-Parameters: alwaysuploadlogs \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes,REQFAIL=100,DURATION=28800 \
clientdistro=el6 ossdistro=el6 mdsdistro=el6 clientarch=x86_64 \
ossarch=x86_64 mdsarch=x86_64 clientcount=4 osscount=2 mdscount=2 \
mdssizegb=10 ostsizegb=10 austeroptions=-R failover=true iscsi=1 \
testlist=recovery-mds-scale
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/8071
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
